title: Sanji Feeds a Starving Enemy
description: On pages 15–18, Sanji feeds Gin despite the danger, revealing that his compassion for the hungry matters more to him than restaurant rules or pirate labels.
scene: Gin arrives broken and starving, while the Baratie cooks judge him as an enemy; Sanji alone treats hunger as something that must be answered.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Then like a herd of elephants stomping on an ant,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This restaurant will be crushed!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I see...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Then you're suggesting we should've given him a warm welcome?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Don't you think that's a little rude to our other guests!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: As a fighting cook that baratie is renowned for,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I am ashamed by such an idea!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Just how many pirates have we chased off until now!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If yer so scared, then why don'cha just quit!

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: What!? It's precisely because of those words all the waiters quit!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: This is all your fault that we're so busy, Patty!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hmph! As if we ever needed those spineless sacks of crap in the first place!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey, you blockheads!

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.text: Get back to work!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yessir!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: See ya then.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I just wanted to warn you.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But I'm still gonna go to grand line!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Haha... well, you are free to do what you want. It's not like I have the right to stop that.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And Sanji,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Thank you so much for the food. You're my Saviour.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That meal was really the best I've ever had.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Would it be alright to come here to eat again?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Sure, anytime.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey errand-boy! So that's where you were!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Agh! old man!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Go, gin...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Ah, sorry. Just because you gave me food,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You're going to get scolded...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Come again?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: How can I be scolded,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If there's no proof?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Don't you get caught again, gin!

summary: This part reveals the core of Sanji’s morality: no matter who a person is, he cannot ignore someone starving in front of him.
